free_text: Scattering of Neutrons from O16 in the 2.2- to 4.2-MeV
Energy Range
Tabulated angular distributions
1.12-in.-diam. by 1-in.-long beryllium-oxide cylinder,
initially, and liquid-oxygen thin-walled Dewar, later.
Stilbene crystal (1 in. diam by 5/8 in. long).
Monoenergetic neutrons were produced by bombarding
zirconium-tritium targets with protons from the ORNL
5.5 MV Van de Graaff machine.
The beryllium-oxide sample was placed halfway
between the source and the detector. Scattered neutrons
were detected by stilbene crystal. The angular position
of the detector could be changed by rotating the
circular mounting table in the neutron scattering
chamber. Pulses arising from gamma rays were
supressed by pulse shape discrimination. For each
angular measurement differential cross sections were
deduced.
Experimental differential cross sections were
least-square fitted to the data with the phase shifts
and resonance parameters. The phase shift analysis
was performed with the program ANNA. A subroutine of
the program calculated differential cross sections
for neutron scattering. The main program averages the
theoretical cross sections from this subroutine over
energy spread of the measurements.
The cross sections have been corrected for room
scattering background and for in-scattering, for
self-attenuation, for finite angular resolution, and
for energy dependence of the detector efficiency.
